title: Create a Permission Group (Team)
lede: Permission Groups are the teams that power your human contact center — Sales, Support, Spanish line, VIP desk. Create a group, add your people, and every phone number, ring rule, and queue you set up later plugs straight into it. This guide takes you from an empty Groups tab to a fully staffed team.

steps.title: Open the Groups tab
steps.content:
Everything about your human teams lives in one place:

  1. In the left sidebar, click Contact Center (under the Call Center section). You can also reach it from the top navigation: Contact Center.
  2. Click the Groups tab at the top of the page.

The screen splits in two:

  • Groups (left column) — the list of your Permission Groups, each showing its name, description, and a member count.
  • Group (right panel) — the selected group's members and all of its call-center settings, organized as inner tabs.
⚠️ The Groups tab is only visible to the main account and members whose Contact Center role is Admin. If you don't see it, ask an Admin to raise your role — see Members & Roles.
💡 Hover How does this work? next to the Permission Groups title for a quick reminder: besides call routing, groups also power data sharing — a sub-account with team-wide visibility sees records owned by anyone in their groups.

images.alt: Groups tab overview with the group list on the left and the group panel on the right
images.caption: The Groups tab: group list on the left, member and settings panel on the right.

images.alt: Groups tab overview with the group list on the left and the group panel on the right
images.caption: The Groups tab: group list on the left, member and settings panel on the right.

steps.title: Create a new group
steps.content:
1. Click New Group in the top-right corner.
  2. Enter a Name — pick something your team will instantly recognize, like Sales Team or Spanish Support.
  3. Optionally fill in Description (optional) to note what the group represents.
  4. Click Create. The group appears in the left list and is selected automatically, ready for members.
💡 Create one group per team or skill — for example separate Sales and Support groups — so each can get its own phone numbers, ring strategy, and hold experience later.

images.alt: New Group modal with Name and Description fields
images.caption: The New Group modal — only a name is required.

images.alt: New Group modal with Name and Description fields
images.caption: The New Group modal — only a name is required.

steps.title: Select, rename, or delete a group
steps.content:
The left Groups column is your control panel for existing teams:

  • Click a group to select it — its members and settings load in the right panel.
  • Pencil icon — opens the Edit Group modal to rename the group or change its description, then click Save.
  • Trash icon — deletes the group after you confirm Delete this group?.
💡 Deleting a group only unlinks its members — their user accounts, data, and roles are untouched. Any phone numbers routed to the group stop ringing it, though, so re-point those first.

images.alt: Groups list with member counts and edit and delete icons
images.caption: Each group row shows its member count plus edit (pencil) and delete (trash) actions.

images.alt: Groups list with member counts and edit and delete icons
images.caption: Each group row shows its member count plus edit (pencil) and delete (trash) actions.

images.alt: Edit Group modal with the Name and Description fields pre-filled
images.caption: Edit Group: rename the team or update its description, then click Save.

images.alt: Edit Group modal with the Name and Description fields pre-filled
images.caption: Edit Group: rename the team or update its description, then click Save.

steps.title: Add existing users as members
steps.content:
Now staff the team with people who already have an iMash login:

  1. With the group selected, make sure you're on its Members tab (the first inner tab).
  2. Click Add Members in the top-right of the panel.
  3. In the Add Members modal, search users by name or email and pick one or more — both the main account and sub-accounts can be added.
  4. Click Add. Members appear in the table, each tagged Main or Sub.

To remove someone later, click the red trash icon on their row and confirm Remove from group? — this only removes them from the team, it never deletes their account.

💡 A user can belong to several groups at once — perfect for a bilingual rep who covers both the English and Spanish lines.

images.alt: Add Members modal with a user search field
images.caption: Add Members: search by name or email and pick as many users as you need.

images.alt: Add Members modal with a user search field
images.caption: Add Members: search by name or email and pick as many users as you need.

images.alt: Members tab showing the member table with Main and Sub tags
images.caption: The Members tab: every member is listed with a Main or Sub tag and a remove action.

images.alt: Members tab showing the member table with Main and Sub tags
images.caption: The Members tab: every member is listed with a Main or Sub tag and a remove action.

steps.title: Create a brand-new agent in one step
steps.content:
Hiring someone who doesn't have an iMash login yet? Skip User Management entirely — create them right inside the group:

  1. On the group's Members tab, click Create User (next to Add Members).
  2. Fill in the new agent's details:
    • Email — their login email.
    • Password — at least 8 characters; share it with them securely.
    • First Name and Last Name.
    • Contact Center role — Agent, Monitor / QA, Supervisor, or Admin (see Members & Roles for what each can do).
  3. Click Create User.

The account is created auto-approved — no email confirmation needed — and dropped straight into this group. Its data-access role and view profile follow the group's Role Mapping for the Contact Center role you picked, so the new agent lands with exactly the right permissions on day one.

images.alt: Create User modal with email, password, name, and Contact Center role fields
images.caption: Create User: a ready-to-work agent account in one modal.

images.alt: Create User modal with email, password, name, and Contact Center role fields
images.caption: Create User: a ready-to-work agent account in one modal.

steps.title: Understand Online vs. Offline (who actually rings)
steps.content:
Being a member is only half the story — group calls ring online members only. Each member controls their own availability from the web dialer (the phone icon in the top-right of the app), under My status:

  • Online — they are rung for AI transfers to the group, inbound calls to the group's numbers, and queued callers.
  • On Break — temporarily out of the ring rotation; the status sticks until they change it.
  • Offline — not rung at all.
  • Busy on a Call / Busy — Wrap-up — set automatically while a member is on a live call or logging the call outcome; you can't select it manually, and no new calls ring in during it.

If nobody in the group is online, the group's fallback kicks in — typically the AI agent takes the call (configured on the Inbound SIP Routing tab).

💡 Worried agents will close the dialer and miss calls? On the group's Members Roles tab, use Keep the web dialer always open for to pin the dialer open for chosen roles so they never miss a queued call.

images.alt: My status selector in the web dialer set to Online
images.caption: My status in the web dialer — Online members are the ones who ring.

images.alt: My status selector in the web dialer set to Online
images.caption: My status in the web dialer — Online members are the ones who ring.

steps.title: Tour the group's inner tabs
steps.content:
With people on board, the group's inner tab bar is your setup checklist. Each tab has its own detailed guide:

  • Members — the roster you just built (this guide).
  • Members Roles — set each member's Contact Center role (Agent, Monitor / QA, Supervisor, Admin), pin the dialer open per role, build the group's Skills catalog (tag members with skills like Spanish or Billing that agents can filter by when transferring), and manage post-call Dispositions. → Members & Roles
  • Role Mapping — map each Contact Center role to a data-access role and view profile that apply automatically on save. This tab only appears for the main account and sub-accounts with User Management enabled. → Role Mapping
  • Inbound SIP Routing — point phone numbers straight at this group and choose the fallback when nobody answers. → Inbound SIP Routing
  • Ring strategy — ring everyone at once, round-robin, linear, or longest-idle, plus ring timeouts and per-call behavior like auto-answer. → Ring Strategy
  • Wait & Hold experience — wait music, hold music, and periodic announcements callers hear. → Wait & Hold
  • Queue Settings — max wait time and what happens when the queue times out. → Queue Settings

One Save button in the bottom-right commits every settings tab at once — you can adjust several tabs, then save a single time. (It's hidden on Members, where adding and removing members applies instantly.)

Start with Members Roles, then Inbound SIP Routing — the moment a number points at your group, your team is live and taking calls. 🎉

images.alt: The group's inner tab bar: Members, Members Roles, Role Mapping, Inbound SIP Routing, Ring strategy, Wait and Hold experience, Queue Settings
images.caption: The group's inner tabs — each one is a step in your team's call setup.

images.alt: The group's inner tab bar: Members, Members Roles, Role Mapping, Inbound SIP Routing, Ring strategy, Wait and Hold experience, Queue Settings
images.caption: The group's inner tabs — each one is a step in your team's call setup.

support.content:
